verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II membrane-bound glycosyltransferase resident in the Golgi apparatus. The N-terminal hydrophobic signal sequence remains uncleaved and functions as a transmembrane domain, anchoring the protein with its catalytic domain facing the Golgi lumen. This is canonical endomembrane (intra-Golgi) localization. No evidence for: cell-state-induced cancer/cell-death ecto-pool, tissue-restricted germline/developmental surface display, lysosomal exocytosis biology, PM cycling or dual localization, or stable covalent/wash-resistant surface tethering. Glycosyltransferases in this family are housekeeping biosynthetic enzymes with no documented pMHC or surface-directed therapeutic targeting. The protein body does not reach the extracellular face.
